user_query:
I keep a tiny folder on my computer with the runners’ training session logs and a simple pipeline config. I’d like a reliable, local version of my weekly report. Please do the following using only the files in this workspace and save all outputs under the specified paths:

1) Compute summary metrics from data/sessions.csv and write them to output/metrics.json with these exact fields:
   - sessions (integer count of rows)
   - total_distance_km (sum of distance_km)
   - avg_distance_km (total_distance_km divided by sessions, rounded to two decimals)
   - total_water_liters (sum of water_liters)
   - total_snacks_bars (sum of snacks_bars)
   - top_runner (runner with the highest total distance; if there is a tie, pick the alphabetically first name)
   - top_runner_total_km (that runner’s total distance)

2) Create a human-readable summary at output/summary.md listing those same values, each on its own line in the form "Label: value" (e.g., "Sessions: 8", "Top runner: Ana (23.5 km)").

3) Update docs/README.md by replacing the placeholder text "Latest Hydration Summary: [TO_BE_FILLED]" with a single line in this format that reflects the numbers you computed: "Latest Hydration Summary: {sessions} sessions, {total_distance_km} km total, {total_water_liters} L water, top runner: {top_runner}."

4) Inspect and correct ci/pipeline.yaml so that the build-report step is enabled and points to the correct paths: set enabled to true for the step named "build-report", set inputs.data to "data/sessions.csv", set outputs.metrics to "output/metrics.json", and set outputs.summary to "output/summary.md". Do not change anything else in that file.

Deliverables:
- output/metrics.json (structured as specified, values computed from data/sessions.csv)
- output/summary.md (lines matching the metrics)
- docs/README.md (placeholder replaced with the computed one-line summary)
- ci/pipeline.yaml (edited as described)

Please make sure the numbers in README and summary.md exactly match the values in output/metrics.json. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
